_meta.description: Canonical slot definitions for the Excel Calculation Agent with validation rules, coercion logic, and default values for 68 slot types.

slots.description: List of allowed values for data validation constraints.
Direction of alternative hypothesis in statistical tests.
Starting value for growth rate / CAGR calculations.
Beta coefficient of an asset relative to the market (CAPM).
Number of bins for histograms or binning operations.
Custom bin boundary values for discretization.
Method for creating bins.
Cell range containing a series of cash flows (positive = inflow, negative = outflow).
Column input cell for a two-variable data table.
Column substitute values for a two-variable data table.
Number of components to retain in decomposition (SVD, PCA).
Confidence level for intervals and tests (e.g., 0.95 for 95%).
Constraint definitions for solver/optimization problems.
Type of correlation to compute.
Annual coupon rate of a bond as a decimal.
Whether to compute the cumulative distribution (CDF) vs the density (PDF/PMF).
Custom Excel formula for validation rules.
Date format string for type coercion.
Cell range containing date values for time series operations.
Day count convention for bond pricing.
Pre-tax cost of debt for WACC calculation.
Proportion of debt in capital structure.
Decimal separator for number parsing.
Declining balance factor (e.g., 2 for double declining).
Type of seasonal decomposition.
Degrees of freedom for the distribution or test.
Denominator degrees of freedom (F-distribution).
Numerator degrees of freedom (F-distribution).
Cell range for the dependent (Y) variable in regression.
Original cost (basis) of the asset for depreciation.
Whether the distribution is discrete (vs continuous).
Distribution-specific parameters (passed as key-value pairs).
Distribution specifications for each variable in Monte Carlo simulation.
Type of probability distribution.
Continuous dividend yield for option pricing.
Expected effect size for power analysis and sample size estimation.
Type of effect size measure to compute.
Ending value for CAGR / growth rate calculations.
Cost of equity for WACC calculation.
Proportion of equity in capital structure.
Cell range containing expected frequencies for chi-square goodness of fit.
Extra payment per period in amortization schedules.
Face (par) value of a bond.
Column containing Factor A levels for two-way ANOVA.
Column containing Factor B levels for two-way ANOVA.
Total fixed costs for break-even analysis.
Method for forecasting.
Number of future periods to forecast.
Cell containing the formula to evaluate or optimize.
Column containing group labels for ANOVA or grouped analysis.
Type of growth rate calculation.
Initial guess for iterative rate calculations (IRR, RATE).
Whether to exclude zero values from calculations.
Method for filling missing values.
Whether to include an intercept term in the regression model.
Initial investment amount (usually negative for outflow).
Input cell for one-variable data table.
Primary cell range containing the input data.
Secondary cell range for two-variable operations (correlation, t-tests, matrix ops).
Interest/discount rate per period as a decimal.
Method for interpolation.
Number of iterations for Monte Carlo or iterative methods.
Which duplicate to keep during deduplication.
Columns to use as deduplication keys.
L1/L2 mixing ratio for elastic net (0=ridge, 1=lasso).
Number of lag periods for autocorrelation or lag analysis.
Lambda (mean rate) parameter for the Poisson distribution.
Mean of the natural log of the variable (log-normal distribution).
Standard deviation of the natural log of the variable (log-normal distribution).
Expected market return for CAPM.
Time to maturity in years for bonds.
Maximum iterations for goal seek or solver.
Maximum lag for autocorrelation function.
Maximum allowed value or upper bound.
Whether to maximize (true) or minimize (false) the objective.
Minimum allowed value or lower bound.
Whether to return all modes (multi-modal) or just the first.
Method for data normalization.
Number of events (k) for Poisson probability.
Number of periods (NPER) for TVM calculations.
Number of successes (k) for binomial probability.
Number of trials (n) for binomial distribution.
Cell containing the objective function value to optimize.
Cell range containing observed frequencies for chi-square goodness of fit.
Type of option for Black-Scholes pricing.
Method for outlier detection.
Target cell where the calculation result should be placed.
Periodic payment amount for TVM calculations.
Frequency of coupon/payment periods.
Whether payments occur at the beginning or end of each period.
Percentile interpolation method (inclusive vs exclusive).
The percentile to compute (0-1 or 0-100).
Window size (number of periods) for moving average or rolling calculations.
Specific period number for depreciation calculation.
Type of period for period-over-period comparison.
Degree of the polynomial for polynomial regression.
Known or hypothesized population mean for tests.
Whether to compute the population or sample statistic.
Total population size for finite population correction.
Known population standard deviation.
X value at which to make a regression prediction.
Number of predictor variables for adjusted R-squared.
Present value or loan principal for TVM calculations.
Selling price per unit for break-even analysis.
A probability value between 0 and 1.
Which quartile to compute (1, 2, or 3).
Rank order direction.
Rate parameter (λ) for the exponential distribution.
Regularization strength parameter for ridge/lasso/elastic net.
Reinvestment rate for MIRR calculation.
Condition to reset cumulative sum (e.g., per-group or per-period).
Cells containing results to track in scenario analysis.
Risk-free rate of return (e.g., treasury rate).
Row input cell for a two-variable data table.
Row substitute values for a two-variable data table.
Salvage value of an asset at end of useful life.
Number of observations or sample size.
Scale parameter for gamma, Weibull, or other distributions.
Scenario definitions with names and variable values.
Length of the seasonal cycle (e.g., 12 for monthly, 4 for quarterly).
Random seed for reproducible simulations.
Alpha (shape) parameter for beta, gamma, or Weibull distributions.
Beta (shape) parameter for the beta distribution.
Significance level (alpha) for hypothesis tests.
Smoothing factor (alpha) for exponential smoothing (0 < α ≤ 1).
Trend smoothing factor (beta) for double exponential smoothing.
Solver algorithm for nonlinear optimization.
Current spot price of the underlying asset (Black-Scholes).
Statistic to compute in rolling window operations.
Desired statistical power (1 - β) for sample size estimation.
Number of steps for sensitivity analysis.
Strike (exercise) price of an option.
Substitute values for what-if / data table analysis.
Probability of success on a single trial (binomial).
Tail type for t-distribution probability.
Target maximum value for normalization.
Target minimum value for normalization.
Target profit for break-even analysis beyond zero.
Minimum acceptable return for Sortino ratio.
Target data type for type coercion.
Target value for goal seek.
X value at which to interpolate.
Corporate tax rate for WACC calculation.
A pre-computed test statistic value for p-value calculation.
Type of statistical test for power analysis.
Classification threshold for logistic regression.
Threshold for outlier detection (e.g., z-score cutoff, IQR multiplier).
Method for handling tied values in ranking.
Time to option expiration in years.
Convergence tolerance for iterative methods.
Type of trend to fit.
Whether to trim (remove) or winsorize (clamp) extreme values.
Percentage of values to trim from each tail (0 to 0.5).
Useful life of an asset in periods (years).
Type of data validation to apply.
Cell containing the variable to adjust in goal seek.
Cells containing decision variables for solver/optimization.
Variable cost per unit for break-even analysis.
Percentage variation for sensitivity analysis (e.g., +/- 10%).
Annualized volatility (standard deviation of returns) for option pricing.
Cell range containing weights for weighted calculations.
The x value at which to evaluate a distribution function.
